name: Hotel Cala di Volpe, a Luxury Collection Hotel

content.verdict: Cala di Volpe is the founding legend of the Costa Smeralda — the pink-stuccoed, tower-studded village the Aga Khan commissioned in 1963 that still dictates what Italian summer luxury looks like. For a honeymoon it is theatrical in the best sense: you eat pasta alla bottarga next to film producers on the Barbecue terrace, float in the 42m seawater pool carved into the hillside, and sleep in rooms where hand-painted headboards and terracotta floors feel both rustic and absurdly expensive. It is not the quietest hotel in Sardinia, nor the cheapest — but for couples who want to be at the center of Costa Smeralda's social universe, nothing else on the island matches its magnetism.
content.best_room: Book a Premium Sea View Room in the main building for balcony views over the Cala di Volpe bay and the hotel's own yacht marina. For splurge budgets, the Presidential Suite Penthouse with private rooftop pool is the most photographed honeymoon room in the Mediterranean — but the one-bedroom Suite Sea View gives you 80% of the magic at half the price.

itinerary_7_nights.title: Arrival and Barbecue terrace dinner
itinerary_7_nights.description: Transfer 30 minutes from Olbia Costa Smeralda airport. Check in, settle into your sea-view room, and head straight to the Sunday-night Barbecue buffet on the terrace — a Costa Smeralda institution since 1967 and the best way to see who is on the Riviera this week.

itinerary_7_nights.title: Seawater pool and Beefbar lunch
itinerary_7_nights.description: Spend the morning at the hotel's dramatic saltwater pool, then walk over to Beefbar Cala di Volpe (the Monte Carlo import) for a long wagyu lunch. Late afternoon spa treatment for two at the Shiseido Spa.

itinerary_7_nights.title: Private boat to La Maddalena
itinerary_7_nights.description: Charter one of the hotel's Frauscher speedboats with skipper. Cruise to Spargi, Budelli's Pink Beach viewpoint, and anchor for a swim in the translucent shallows of Cala Corsara. Lunch on board from the hotel's picnic menu.

itinerary_7_nights.title: Porto Cervo village and shopping
itinerary_7_nights.description: Stroll Porto Cervo's piazzetta in the morning, browse the Promenade du Port boutiques, lunch at Phi Beach's sister restaurant Quattro Passi, then a sunset aperitivo at the hotel's Pontile Bar overlooking the yachts.

itinerary_7_nights.title: Beach day at Capriccioli and Liscia Ruja
itinerary_7_nights.description: The concierge arranges sunbeds and a beach car to the two most romantic coves nearby. Lunch at PhiBeach's Tavolara. Back to the pool for sunset champagne before dinner.

itinerary_7_nights.title: Pevero Golf Club and dinner at Novikov
itinerary_7_nights.description: Morning nine holes at the Robert Trent Jones–designed Pevero (or a simple walk around the cliffside course). Late afternoon in-room massage. Dinner at the hotel's Novikov outpost for Pan-Asian fireworks.

itinerary_7_nights.title: Farewell breakfast and Olbia departure
itinerary_7_nights.description: Leisurely breakfast on the terrace, a last swim, and the concierge car to the airport. Many couples add two nights inland at a Gallura agriturismo to decompress before flying home.

caveats: The hotel only operates from late April to early October — book July and August at least 9 months out as every sea-view room sells out.
It is family-friendly in peak season; if you want adults-only calm, come in late May, June, or late September.
Costa Smeralda pricing is eye-watering in August (easily 3x June rates). Shoulder season gives you the same weather at half the cost.

faqs.question: Is Cala di Volpe adults-only?
faqs.answer: No — it has a kids' club and families are welcome, though the atmosphere remains sophisticated. Honeymooners who want quiet should book June or late September.

faqs.question: How do we get there from the airport?
faqs.answer: Olbia Costa Smeralda (OLB) is 30 minutes by car. The hotel arranges private transfers in Mercedes S-Class or Alfa Romeo for around 180 euros each way.

faqs.question: Which months are best for a honeymoon?
faqs.answer: Mid-June and early September are the sweet spots: warm sea, long evenings, lively but not chaotic. July and August are spectacular but crowded and priced accordingly.

faqs.question: Is the beach private?
faqs.answer: The hotel has a reserved beach area on Cala di Volpe bay with attendants, umbrellas, and water sports — included for guests. The beach itself is technically public (all Italian beaches are).

faqs.question: Do we need a car?
faqs.answer: Not strictly — the hotel runs a free shuttle to Porto Cervo and nearby beaches. But a rental or chauffeur gives you flexibility to explore quieter Gallura beaches inland.

faqs.question: What is the dress code at dinner?
faqs.answer: Smart elegant in all restaurants. Men wear linen trousers and a collared shirt; no shorts after 7 pm. The Barbecue terrace is the most formal — pack one nice dress and one jacket.

content.email_template:
Subject: Honeymoon booking inquiry — Hotel Cala di Volpe

Buongiorno,

My partner and I are planning our honeymoon for [DATES] and would love to stay 5 to 7 nights at Cala di Volpe. Could you please share availability and best-available rates for a Premium Sea View Room or Junior Suite Sea View?

A few questions:
1. Do you have honeymoon packages that include breakfast, a spa treatment, or a celebratory dinner?
2. Is late checkout on our departure day possible?
3. Can you arrange a half-day private boat with skipper to La Maddalena archipelago?

We are celebrating our wedding and any romantic touches (rose petals, champagne on arrival, a sunset dinner on the Pontile) would be very welcome.

Grazie mille,
[Your names]
